Title: User Registration Success
Preconditions: User is on registration page, has valid email and password
Steps: 1. Navigate to /register | 2. Enter name "John Doe" | 3. Enter email "john@example.com" | 4. Enter password "SecurePass123" | 5. Click Register button
Test Data: Name: John Doe, Email: john@example.com, Password: SecurePass123
Expected Result: User is registered successfully, JWT token is stored in localStorage, user is redirected to /dashboard

Title: User Login Success
Preconditions: User has registered account, is on login page
Steps: 1. Navigate to /login | 2. Enter email "john@example.com" | 3. Enter password "SecurePass123" | 4. Click Sign In button
Test Data: Email: john@example.com, Password: SecurePass123
Expected Result: User is authenticated, JWT token is stored, redirected to /dashboard

Title: Vehicle Registration Success
Preconditions: User is logged in, navigates to /vehicles page
Steps: 1. Click "Add Vehicle" button | 2. Enter license plate "ABC123" | 3. Enter make "Toyota" | 4. Enter model "Camry" | 5. Enter color "Blue" | 6. Click Add Vehicle
Test Data: License: ABC123, Make: Toyota, Model: Camry, Color: Blue
Expected Result: Vehicle is registered and appears in vehicle list with uppercase license plate ABC123

Title: View Parking Spot Availability
Preconditions: User is logged in
Steps: 1. Navigate to /parking-spots | 2. Observe the spots grid | 3. Select Floor 1 filter | 4. Observe filtered results
Test Data: None required
Expected Result: All parking spots are displayed with status (Available/Occupied/Reserved), filtering by floor works correctly, real-time updates occur every 5 seconds

Title: Create Parking Reservation
Preconditions: User is logged in, has registered vehicle, available parking spot exists
Steps: 1. Navigate to /reserve | 2. Select vehicle from dropdown | 3. Select Floor 1 | 4. Select available spot 101 | 5. Set start time to tomorrow 9:00 AM | 6. Set end time to tomorrow 5:00 PM | 7. Click Reserve Spot
Test Data: Vehicle: registered vehicle, Spot: 101, Start: tomorrow 9 AM, End: tomorrow 5 PM
Expected Result: Reservation is created successfully, spot status changes to Reserved, user is redirected to /reservations

Title: Complete User Journey from Registration to Reservation
Preconditions: System is running, MongoDB is connected, no existing user
Steps: 1. Register new user with email test@example.com | 2. Login redirects to dashboard | 3. View real-time occupancy stats | 4. Navigate to /vehicles | 5. Add vehicle Honda Accord ABC456 | 6. Navigate to /reserve | 7. Select vehicle and spot 201 | 8. Create reservation for tomorrow | 9. Navigate to /reservations | 10. View created reservation | 11. Cancel reservation
Test Data: Email: test@example.com, Password: TestPass123, Vehicle: ABC456 Honda Accord, Spot: 201
Expected Result: Complete flow works end-to-end, user can register, login, add vehicle, create reservation, view it, and cancel it without errors

Title: Real-time Occupancy Monitoring Flow
Preconditions: User is logged in at dashboard
Steps: 1. Observe occupancy statistics at dashboard | 2. Wait 5 seconds | 3. Observe data refresh | 4. Check Total/Occupied/Reserved/Available counts | 5. Verify floor-by-floor breakdown | 6. Check occupancy percentage | 7. Verify occupancy bar visualization
Test Data: None required
Expected Result: Dashboard displays live occupancy data, auto-refreshes every 5 seconds, shows correct totals and per-floor stats, visual bar reflects current state accurately

Title: Registration with Short Password
Preconditions: User is on registration page
Steps: 1. Enter name "Test User" | 2. Enter email "test@example.com" | 3. Enter password "short" (only 5 chars) | 4. Click Register
Test Data: Name: Test User, Email: test@example.com, Password: short
Expected Result: Error message displayed: "Password must be at least 8 characters long", registration is prevented, user stays on registration page

Title: Duplicate Vehicle Registration
Preconditions: User is logged in, has already registered vehicle ABC123
Steps: 1. Navigate to /vehicles | 2. Click Add Vehicle | 3. Enter same license plate "ABC123" | 4. Enter different make/model | 5. Click Add Vehicle
Test Data: License: ABC123, Make: Honda, Model: Civic
Expected Result: Error message displayed: "Vehicle with this license plate already registered", duplicate vehicle is not created

Title: Reserve Already Reserved Spot
Preconditions: User is logged in, spot 101 is already reserved
Steps: 1. Navigate to /reserve | 2. Select vehicle | 3. Try to select spot 101 from dropdown | 4. Observe available spots list
Test Data: Spot 101 is reserved by another user
Expected Result: Spot 101 does not appear in available spots dropdown, only unreserved spots are shown, no reservation conflict occurs

Title: Access Dashboard Without Authentication
Preconditions: User is not logged in, no token in localStorage
Steps: 1. Directly navigate to /dashboard URL | 2. Observe redirect behavior
Test Data: None required
Expected Result: User is immediately redirected to /login page, dashboard content is not displayed, authentication is enforced

Title: Reservation Time Conflict
Preconditions: User tries to reserve spot already reserved for overlapping time
Steps: 1. Navigate to /reserve | 2. Select spot 102 | 3. Set start/end times that overlap with existing reservation | 4. Submit reservation
Test Data: Spot 102 has reservation from 9 AM to 5 PM, user selects 3 PM to 7 PM same day
Expected Result: Error message displayed: "Parking spot already reserved for this time period", reservation is not created

Title: View All Parking Spots Without Login
Preconditions: User is not logged in
Steps: 1. Navigate to /parking-spots URL
Test Data: None required
Expected Result: System either redirects to login (if protected) or shows spots data publicly (per system design). Based on code, user is redirected to /login

Title: Filter Parking Spots by Multiple Floors
Preconditions: User is on /parking-spots page
Steps: 1. Click "Floor 1" filter button | 2. Count displayed spots | 3. Click "Floor 2" button | 4. Count displayed spots | 5. Click "All Floors" button | 6. Verify all spots shown
Test Data: None required
Expected Result: Each floor filter shows only spots for that floor (20 spots per floor expected), All Floors shows all 60 spots, filtering is instant and accurate

Title: View Reservation History
Preconditions: User has multiple reservations (active and cancelled)
Steps: 1. Navigate to /reservations | 2. Observe list of all reservations | 3. Check status badges | 4. Verify start/end times format
Test Data: User has 3 reservations: 1 active, 2 cancelled
Expected Result: All reservations displayed in reverse chronological order, status badges show correct state (active/cancelled), date-time formatted as locale string

Title: Cancel Active Reservation
Preconditions: User has active reservation
Steps: 1. Navigate to /reservations | 2. Find active reservation | 3. Click "Cancel Reservation" button | 4. Confirm cancellation dialog | 5. Observe result
Test Data: Active reservation for spot 103
Expected Result: Confirmation dialog appears, upon confirming reservation status changes to cancelled, reservation removed from active list, spot becomes available again

Title: Dashboard Occupancy Bar Visualization
Preconditions: Dashboard is loaded, parking spots have mixed status (some occupied, some reserved, some available)
Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard | 2. Locate occupancy bar | 3. Observe colored segments | 4. Verify segment widths match percentages
Test Data: Assume 10 occupied, 15 reserved, 35 available out of 60 total
Expected Result: Occupancy bar shows red segment (occupied) at ~17%, orange segment (reserved) at ~25%, remaining is empty (available ~58%), visual proportions are accurate

Title: Logout Functionality
Preconditions: User is logged in at dashboard
Steps: 1. Click "Logout" button | 2. Observe redirect
Test Data: None required
Expected Result: JWT token is removed from localStorage, user is redirected to /login, attempting to access protected routes redirects back to login

Title: Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: User is on login page, account does not exist or wrong password
Steps: 1. Enter email "wrong@example.com" | 2. Enter password "wrongpass" | 3. Click Sign In
Test Data: Email: wrong@example.com, Password: wrongpass
Expected Result: Error message displayed: "Invalid email or password" or similar, login is prevented, no token is stored

Title: Register with Duplicate Email
Preconditions: User tries to register with email already in system
Steps: 1. Navigate to /register | 2. Enter name "Duplicate User" | 3. Enter email "john@example.com" (already exists) | 4. Enter valid password | 5. Click Register
Test Data: Name: Duplicate User, Email: john@example.com (exists), Password: SecurePass123
Expected Result: Error message displayed: "Email already registered", registration is prevented, no duplicate user created

Title: Submit Reservation Without Vehicle
Preconditions: User is logged in but has not registered any vehicles
Steps: 1. Navigate to /reserve | 2. Observe form state
Test Data: User has 0 vehicles registered
Expected Result: Warning message displayed: "You need to register a vehicle before making a reservation" with link to /vehicles, reservation form is disabled or not shown

Title: Reserve Spot Without Selecting Vehicle
Preconditions: User has vehicles registered, on /reserve page
Steps: 1. Leave vehicle dropdown blank or unselected | 2. Select spot and times | 3. Click Reserve Spot
Test Data: Vehicle: not selected, Spot: 104, Times: valid future times
Expected Result: Client-side or server-side validation error: "Please select a vehicle", reservation is not created

Title: Reserve Spot with Invalid Time Range
Preconditions: User tries to set end time before start time
Steps: 1. Select vehicle and spot | 2. Set start time to tomorrow 5:00 PM | 3. Set end time to tomorrow 2:00 PM (earlier) | 4. Click Reserve Spot
Test Data: Vehicle: selected, Spot: 105, Start: tomorrow 5 PM, End: tomorrow 2 PM (invalid)
Expected Result: Browser HTML5 validation prevents submission or backend returns error: invalid time range, reservation is not created

Title: Access Protected Route After Token Expiry
Preconditions: User was logged in, JWT token has expired (after 24 hours)
Steps: 1. Wait for token expiration or manually expire token | 2. Try to access /dashboard or /vehicles
Test Data: Token is expired
Expected Result: API returns 401 Unauthorized, user is redirected to /login, localStorage token is removed, user must re-authenticate
